page: Amazon Web Services m8id.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores with hyperthreading, powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled as a 237 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the network baseline bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps. The underlying virtualization is managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2052.0 and high single-threaded compression speeds, including 34.06 GB/sec in gzip compression and 14.46 GB/sec in bzip3 decompression. Geekbench text processing is a notable highlight, scoring in the top tier at 2780.0. However, multi-core and multi-threaded performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 4318.27 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 28.9 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is average, but small-block cached read-only memory bandwidth is weak at 321.27 GB/sec.

This instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, single-threaded applications, text processing, and lightweight web serving. The inclusion of a 237 GB NVMe SSD local instance store provides low-latency temporary storage, making it useful for scratch space or caching. However, due to weak multi-core scaling and poor medium-model LLM text generation speeds of 5.03 tokens/sec, it is not recommended for heavy multi-threaded computing, large-scale database operations, or intensive machine learning inference.
description: The Amazon Web Services m8id.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR5 RAM. It features a bundled 237 GB NVMe SSD local instance store and a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, managed by the Nitro hypervisor. Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2052.0 and top-tier text processing performance. However, multi-core scaling is limited, with weak multi-core stress-ng and database operation scores. This resource profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for single-threaded applications, text processing, and lightweight web serving requiring fast local scratch storage, while being less optimal for highly parallelized compute or database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 237 GB NVMe SSD. It offers strong single-core performance but exhibits weaker multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8id.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 237 GB NVMe SSD local instance store.
tagline: A general-purpose instance combining strong single-core performance and fast DDR5 memory with a local NVMe SSD instance store.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB DDR5 RAM
Bundled 237 GB local NVMe SSD instance store for low-latency scratch space
Strong single-core performance and top-tier text processing capabilities
Weaker multi-core scaling and database operation performance
Best suited for single-threaded applications, text processing, and web serving
